rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AVERY WRIGHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/23/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The real property was appraised at $975,000, not $700,000. (2) The bond calculation does not appear to be correct: 1% of $682,652.90 = $6,826.53. (3) Did it really take the conservator five hours to put together his timesheet on 1/15/24? Does he not track his time as he provides the services? (4) The conservator's and attorneys' fees ordered on 4/22/24 do not appear to have been paid during this accounting period, but Schedule H does not reflect any liabilities. (5) Has the successor conservator of the estate consented to the dual representation by the attorney for the former conservator? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/9/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM COLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. No petition for final distribution or report of status of administration with a request for additional time has been filed yet, as required by Probate Code Section 12200 and as ordered on 4/9/25, OVER A YEAR AGO. No Final I&A has been filed yet as required to be done within four months after Letters are first issued to a general personal representative. [Prob. C. Sect. 8800(b).] Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. EXAMINERS WILL NOT RESPOND TO ONE PARTY, WHEN A PARTY WHO REQUESTED SPECIAL NOTICE IS NOT INCLUDED IN THE COMMUNICATION.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARNOLD YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/23/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "amended third petition" (which is actually a third amended first and final report and petition for final distribution) was e-filed on 4/24/26. No proof of service of notice of this erroneously captioned petition has been filed. (An amended pleading is a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes [CRC 7.3(3).]; it should not "address" issues raised by an examiner.) (2) No notice of this third amended petition appears to have been given. (3) The request for the attorney's fees for extraordinary services does not appear to have been supported by a fee declaration. [CRC 7.702.] A complete and correct THIRD AMENDED PETITION FOR FINAL DISTRIBUTION, etc., must be filed that is a pleading that stands on its own and is not a "response" to issues. (The rest of this "amended third petition" has not been reviewed, because it is not a proper and complete petition for final distribution.) Please provide a courtesy copy of the third amended petition for final distribution and a revised proposed order, if applicable, and any other new filed papers to the assigned examiner by 6/9/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] Proposed orders in Probate proceedings do not require a cover sheet; they should just be provided to the examiner.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAROLD F. POHL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/16/2026. Issue necessitating continuance: A disclaimer is conclusively presumed to have been filed with a reasonable time if it is filed within nine months after the death of the creator of the interest or within nine months after the interest becomes indefeasibly vested, whichever occurs later (Probate Code Section 279(b)(1)). If the disclaimer is not filed within the time provided, the disclaimant has the burden of establishing that the disclaimer was filed within a reasonable time (Probate Code Section 279(f)). In a verified supplement, please clarify how the disclaimer was timely filed, as it was not filed or otherwise presented to the court until May 4, 2026, despite having been executed in November of 2024. Please address the above issue and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 06/02/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANA BERTHA ACOSTA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/24/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) If an estate is not ready to close within the statutory time set forth in Probate Code Section 12200, the report of status of administration must set forth the status of the estate, including, but not limited to, allegations regarding notice pursuant to Probate Code Section 9202, disclosure of all creditor claims and their dispositions, requests for special notice, and any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution (LRSF 14.35.J.1). 2) The instant petition does not include a prayer for relief. For the Court to make an order, the report and petition must include a prayer specifying the relief sought (LRSF 14.35.J.2). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 06/10/10/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/30/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 2) No Notice of Hearing (Form DE-120) with complete proof of service is on file with the court (Probate Code Section 17203). 3) The petition requests the notary, Wyman Howard, be directed to pay costs incurred, but no notice was given to Wyman Howard. Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 06/16/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BENITA YOLANDA SIEGEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/24/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) No Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Form DE-147S) has been filed (San Francisco Local Rule 14.18.E). 2) No proof of publication has been filed (Probate Code Sections 8120 and 8121). 3) No Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (Form DE-121) has been filed (Probate Code Section 8100). All notices must include language about remote appearances (LRSF 14.1.C.6). 4) The San Francisco Public Administrator has higher priority to serve but was not given notice of the instant petition, and their declination is not on file with the court. 5) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and delivery courtesy copies of all further filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 06/10/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court will determine on petition. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGETTE KATHLEEN SCARDINA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/9/2026. The following issues need to be cleared before 5/29/26. (1) Petition #8 is silent about the petitioner's relationship to the decedent. Also, Katherine F's relationship to the decedent is merely indicated as a cousin, which seems insufficient. Please provide who/how her parent is related to the decedent. All heirs other than Katherine F. must be listed and noticed, if applicable. (2) Petition #8 must indicate the date of death of all deceased heirs, including the decedent's parents. See Local Rule 14.17.D. (3) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate [DE-121] must set forth that the decedent's will is being offered for probate. Notice on file failed to indicate the request in #4. Re-Notice must be given. Also, Notice of Direction for Remote Appearances and Access (LRSF 14.1.C.6) to be given to the persons/organizations who are entitled to. (4) Proof of publication not on file. Publication must include that the petition seeks will admission. (5) A minimum bond of $20,000 is required for a non-California resident personal representative under Local Rule 14.16.E. Petitioner's verified supplement requesting the minimum bond needs to be filed.
